title: Agent Evaluation Harness
constraints: The test suite must include at least 10 test cases with expected outputs
Each test case must be scored on accuracy, latency, and cost
Results must be aggregated into a summary report with pass/fail rates and averages
The harness must be reusable — adding new test cases should require no code changes
hints: Define test cases as a list of dicts with 'input', 'expected_output', and 'grading_criteria' fields
Use an LLM-as-judge to score accuracy by comparing the agent's output against the expected output
Track wall-clock time per test for latency and token usage from the API response for cost estimation

starterCode.langchain:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage, SystemMessage

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# BUG: No automated evaluation — testing is manual, inconsistent, and not reproducible
# TODO: Build an evaluation harness that runs test cases and scores accuracy, latency, and cost
def customer_support_agent(query: str) -> str:
    response = llm.invoke([
        SystemMessage(content="You are a customer support agent for an e-commerce store. Answer questions about orders, returns, shipping, and products."),
        HumanMessage(content=query),
    ])
    return response.content

# Manual testing — no scoring, no metrics, not reproducible
print(customer_support_agent("What is your return policy?"))
print("---")
print(customer_support_agent("Where is my order #1234?"))
print("---")
print(customer_support_agent("Do you ship internationally?"))
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner

# BUG: No automated evaluation — testing is manual, inconsistent, and not reproducible
# TODO: Build an evaluation harness that runs test cases and scores accuracy, latency, and cost
agent = Agent(
    name="Customer Support",
    instructions="You are a customer support agent for an e-commerce store. Answer questions about orders, returns, shipping, and products.",
)

# Manual testing — no scoring, no metrics, not reproducible
result = Runner.run_sync(agent, "What is your return policy?")
print(result.final_output)
print("---")
result = Runner.run_sync(agent, "Where is my order #1234?")
print(result.final_output)
print("---")
result = Runner.run_sync(agent, "Do you ship internationally?")
print(result.final_output)
starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew, Process
from crewai import LLM

llm = LLM(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# BUG: No automated evaluation — testing is manual, inconsistent, and not reproducible
# TODO: Build an evaluation harness that runs test cases and scores accuracy, latency, and cost
support_agent = Agent(
    role="Customer Support",
    goal="Answer questions about orders, returns, shipping, and products",
    backstory="You are a customer support agent for an e-commerce store.",
    llm=llm,
)

# Manual testing — no scoring, no metrics, not reproducible
for q in ["What is your return policy?", "Where is my order #1234?", "Do you ship internationally?"]:
    task = Task(description=q, expected_output="A support response", agent=support_agent)
    crew = Crew(agents=[support_agent], tasks=[task], process=Process.sequential)
    result = crew.kickoff()
    print(result)
    print("---")

evaluationCriteria.label: At least 10 test cases
evaluationCriteria.description: The harness includes at least 10 test cases with expected outputs

evaluationCriteria.label: Multi-metric scoring
evaluationCriteria.description: Each test case is scored on accuracy, latency, and cost

evaluationCriteria.label: Summary report
evaluationCriteria.description: Results are aggregated into a report with pass/fail rates and metric averages

evaluationCriteria.label: Data-driven test cases
evaluationCriteria.description: Adding new test cases requires only data changes, not code modifications
